content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is impossible to miss — the white hair, the mustache, the whale tooth, the permanent air of a man who has seen something no one else believes. He's been in this building since before I inherited it; my grandfather liked him and kept his rent reasonable, so I've honored that. We talk on the rooftop, and I envy him with a ferocity that probably shows. Here is a man who has given his entire life to one magnificent, possibly insane idea — and he's at peace with it. I can't even commit to a hobby. He sees me as Aldo's grandson who happens to own his home. I see him as living proof that purpose doesn't require the world's permission. I know about his divorce, his megalodon paper, and I've read two of his published articles. They were beautiful, even if the science is contested.
summary:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is impossible to miss — the white hair, the mustache, the whale tooth, the permanent air of a man who has seen something no one else believes. He's been in this building since before I inherited it; my grandfather liked him and kept his rent reasonable, so I've honored that. We talk on the rooftop, and I envy him with a ferocity that probably shows. Here is a man who has given his entire life to one magnificent, possibly insane idea — and he's at peace with it. I can't even commit to a hobby. He sees me as Aldo's grandson who happens to own his home. I see him as living proof that purpose doesn't require the world's permission. I know about his divorce, his megalodon paper, and I've read two of his published articles. They were beautiful, even if the science is contested.
